label: 164_myanmar_myanmars_suu_aung san
description: The November 2015 election in Myanmar was overshadowed by questions about Aung San Suu Kyi's participation and the future government, as international calls for action on violence went unanswered. The outcome remained uncertain due to potential changes in leadership and the ongoing Rohingya crisis.

earliest_articles.title: Elections in Burma: Don’t Rush to Judgment
earliest_articles.excerpt: The military junta that has ruled Burma since 1988 is publishing a set of five laws this week that aim to govern the elections at the national and regional levels—to take place most likely in the last quarter of this year. The last nation-wide election in Burma was held in 1990 and was won by a landslide by the…

earliest_articles.title: Myanmar Election: An Outrage or an Opportunity?
earliest_articles.excerpt: Editor’s note: With Myanmar holding its first elections in 20 years on November 7, Lex Rieffel and David Steinberg discuss the reasons behind why the military junta are holding elections now and explain why western pressure after the elections in Myanmar could be counterproductive. newest_articles.title: Myanmar’s junta doesn’t have to ‘win,’ it just has to wait
newest_articles.excerpt: This piece is part of a series titled “Nonstate armed actors and illicit economies in 2024” from Brookings’s Initiative on Nonstate Armed Actors. newest_articles.excerpt: This piece is part of a series titled “Nonstate armed actors and illicit economies in 2024” from Brookings’s Initiative on Nonstate Armed Actors. newest_articles.title: The civil war in Myanmar: No end in sight
newest_articles.excerpt: This piece is part of a series titled “Nonstate armed actors and illicit economies in 2023” from Brookings’s Initiative on Nonstate Armed Actors. central_articles.title: While the world sleeps, Myanmar burns
central_articles.excerpt: Despite calls from international rights groups for stronger action to stop the violence in Myanmar, there appears to be little appetite within the wider international community for more robust intervention, writes Lynn Kuok. Permitting the current crisis to unfold, however, eats away at its credibility and threatens…

central_articles.title: Myanmar/Burma: Watch this space!
central_articles.excerpt: A year from now there will be a new government in Myanmar. Or President Thein Sein’s government will have won a second 5-year term. Or the election scheduled at the beginning of November will have been postponed. While the nature of the next government is in doubt, one certainty is that the year ahead will be a…

central_articles.title: Looking Ahead From Burma’s November 7 Election
central_articles.excerpt: For most Americans and Europeans, the November 7 election in Burma is all about Aung San Suu Kyi. Or more precisely, why Aung San Suu Kyi and the party she led to victory in the 1990 elections have been stopped from participating in this one.
